title: Poultry Selection and Cookery
content:
SELECTION

Young, tender: Firm, moist, smooth skin; plump; bright eyes and comb; soft feet; soft cartilage at end of breast bone.

PREPARATION

Dry-pick before meat stiffens. Hold firmly; grasp feathers; remove with quick, short down-up motion. For goose/duck down: dip quickly in boiling water; rub with hot cloth; or coat with melted paraffin, let harden, scrape off.

Singe: Hold over hot flame few seconds; rub with dry cloth.

Dressing: Cut off head; cut through leg skin below knee; break bone; pull off foot with tendons. Cut slit at back of neck; remove crop and windpipe. Cut slit from breast to vent; remove entrails carefully (avoid breaking gall bladder). Remove gall bladder from liver; discard discolored liver portions. Wash liver, heart, gizzard; wash bird thoroughly inside and out.

COOKERY

Roasting, broiling, steaming, frying for young, tender birds. Roasting and pressure cooking for older birds and large fowl (turkey, goose, duck).

STUFFING

Duck and goose: fruit stuffing. Chicken and turkey: highly seasoned or oyster stuffing. Guinea: rice timbales or hominy grits.
